name: Encoded IEX Detected
description: Detects the use of the "IEX" (Invoke-Expression) cmdlet to execute encoded PowerShell commands. IEX allows running scripts or commands stored in a string, and adversaries may use encoding to conceal their malicious intent. This rule identifies potential attempts to execute encoded PowerShell, a technique used to evade detection.

prompts.prompt: Encoded PowerShell execution via 'Invoke-Expression' (IEX) detected. This could indicate an attempt to conceal malicious commands. Event Details: **Timestamp** {Timestamp}, **Event ID** {EventID}, **User** {user}, **Process Name** {process_name}, **Command** {command}. Investigate this suspicious activity for potential obfuscated malware or command and control communication.

parameters.name: EventID
parameters.description: The Event ID for Process Creation (e.g., 1).

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line containing the encoded IEX pattern.
